methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series C → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.7x) to $2.4B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 7 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Biotech & Health. Range: $600M - $4.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Biotech & Health P/S median is 6.1x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Ultima Genomics
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $4B

private.name: Colossal Biosciences
private.rationale: Same sector (Biotech & Health), same stage (Series C), post-money $1.5B

data.narrative:
Insitro, a Series C biotech company founded by Daphne Koller, has raised $700M+ and holds a current post-money valuation of $2.4B. The valuation assessment draws on a comparable universe of 3 public-market peers (RXRX, TEM, ONT.L) and 8 private-stage companies in Biotech & Health, including Ultima Genomics, Eikon Therapeutics, Colossal Biosciences, Neko Health, and New Limit.

Two triangulation methods apply to this stage and information set. Stage-progression analysis, using a typical 1.7x step-up multiplier from Series C, implies a valuation of $4.1B. Peer-median analysis across 7 same-sector and same/adjacent-stage comparables yields a median of $1.5B, with a range spanning $600M to $4.0B. Public-comp anchoring cannot be applied due to absence of target revenue disclosure, standard for private companies.

The two methods produce a triangulated range of $1.3B (low) to $4.7B (high), with a median of $2.8B. The current $2.4B valuation sits below the median implied by stage-progression but above peer-median guidance, reflecting the wide dispersion in recent biotech valuations at this stage. Key uncertainties include the lack of revenue data for public multiple anchoring and the substantial range across peer valuations ($600M–$4.0B), which reflects heterogeneity in business model and capital efficiency across the peer set.
